edge: Global LP relationships, proprietary data, and execution at very large scale
risk: Fund size may pressure returns; crowded markets can narrow discounts
neo_copy: Data discipline, LP relationship mapping, institutional sourcing process
neo_avoid: Trying to replicate $30bn scale without a proprietary sourcing edge
analyst_note: Ardian is the global liquidity utility model. Neo should study its process, not its size.

edge: Repeatable underwriting, long track record, disciplined portfolio construction
risk: Larger fund sizes can dilute alpha and increase dependence on broad market beta
neo_copy: Standardized underwriting templates, manager scorecards, portfolio-level diligence
neo_avoid: Treating diversified exposure as automatic downside protection
analyst_note: Lexington's value is process consistency. Neo should build repeatable underwriting muscle.

edge: GP access, Blackstone brand, alternatives platform, private wealth reach
risk: Scale pressure and platform conflict perception
neo_copy: Integrate secondaries with broader alternatives and family-office advisory
analyst_note: Strategic Partners shows the power of platform integration. Neo's secondaries strategy should connect with the rest of Neo's alternatives platform.

archetype: Multi-strategy private markets platform with secondaries depth
edge: Primaries, co-investments, and secondaries intelligence in one platform
neo_avoid: Overcomplicating product architecture before sourcing is proven
analyst_note: HarbourVest is useful for Neo because it shows how co-investment and overflow structures can give clients more targeted exposure.

edge: Deep specialist identity and long secondaries history
risk: Competing against larger balance sheets and mega-platforms
analyst_note: Coller is the branding lesson. A secondaries product should be positioned as liquidity intelligence, not another alternatives bucket.

archetype: Institutional-quality secondaries platform with private wealth extension
edge: Co-investment capital, platform credibility, and private wealth access
neo_copy: Package institutional-quality underwriting for family-office clients
neo_avoid: Offering access without sufficient education, transparency, and reporting
analyst_note: AlpInvest is one of the most relevant models for Neo because it bridges institutional secondaries and private wealth access.

edge: Brand, institutional network, private wealth channel, broad alternatives platform
risk: Product distribution can outrun investment differentiation
neo_copy: Client education, clean packaging, and co-investment flexibility
neo_avoid: Selling secondaries as a brand product rather than a portfolio solution
analyst_note: Goldman shows that distribution matters, but Neo should ensure product education is as strong as access.

edge: Asset-level underwriting and GP-led structuring expertise
risk: Concentration risk, GP conflicts, valuation risk, and exit risk
neo_avoid: GP-led deals without conflict, pricing, and exit checks
analyst_note: ICG is the best case study for GP-led secondaries. Neo should copy the diligence depth, not blindly copy concentration.
